summary: Matched and linked `src/melee/it/items/itseakneedleheld.c`, replacing three nonmatching stubs with C implementations, adding the held Sheik needle item state table, typing its item variable owner field, updating prototypes, marking the object Matching, and adjusting local split/symbol metadata for `.sdata` and `.sdata2`.

key_files.role: Primary matched implementation for held Sheik needle item functions and item state table.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/it/items/itseakneedleheld.c hunk replacing stubs with implementations
text_corpus: PR body says all functions in this file match
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports 100% for it_802B18B0, it_802B19AC, itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim, .data, .sdata, .sdata2

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for matched functions, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and broad integer parameters with typed signatures.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: header hunk updates it_802B18B0 and it_802B19AC prototypes

key_files.role: Defines `itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars` with an `owner` field at item variable offset DD4.
Adds `seakneedleheld` to the Item item-variable union.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h adds seakneedleheld union arm

key_files.role: Links the object as Matching instead of NonMatching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py changes itseakneedleheld.c from Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching)

key_files.role: Adjusts `.sdata` and `.sdata2` symbols from global address-style names to local labels for this unit.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt changes it_804D5468, it_804D5470, and it_804DCF88 to local labels @224, @225, and @186
raw_diff: splits.txt changes .sdata end 0x804D5478 to 0x804D5475 and .sdata2 end 0x804DCF90 to 0x804DCF8C

what_changed.text: `it_802B18B0` was implemented to walk held needle JObjs, query the owner fighter's needle count through Sheik or Kirby helper calls, and hide or show up to six needle objects.

`it_802B19AC` was implemented to spawn the held needle item, initialize SpawnItem fields, clear item command vars, store the parent fighter as `seakneedleheld.owner`, and attach the item to a fighter part.

`itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim` was implemented to validate the owner, check Sheik/Kirby special-state helpers, refresh needle visibility, scale the needle JObj from fighter scale, and return the animation result.
A one-entry `ItemStateTable it_803F70A8` was added for the held needle item.
`itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars` and the `seakneedleheld` union arm were added so the owner pointer is accessed as a typed field instead of a raw item-var offset.
The object was switched from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot reports matched code and linked code increases

what_changed.text: Split and symbol metadata were updated for local `.sdata` string symbols and one local `.sdata2` float symbol.

smart_moves.text: Recovered a small typed item-var struct for the held needle owner pointer and threaded it into the main Item union, making all owner accesses explicit.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itCharItems.h adds `Fighter_GObj* owner`; types.h adds `seakneedleheld`; C file uses `ip->xDD4_itemVar.seakneedleheld.owner`

smart_moves.text: Updated the owning header prototypes alongside the matched bodies, including `Item_GObj*`, `Fighter_GObj*`, `Fighter_Part`, and `ItemKind` parameter types.
Used local split/symbol metadata edits rather than adding fake C storage for the `.sdata` and `.sdata2` ownership changes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt local label changes; splits.txt range changes

smart_moves.text: Kept the repeated hide/show logic as a natural `for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)` loop rather than repeated copied blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_802B18B0 loop over six needles

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched item function proves a private item-var field, add the specific ItemVars struct and union arm so accesses use typed fields such as `seakneedleheld.owner`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h adds seakneedleheld union arm
raw_diff: C implementation uses typed owner field

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item code that becomes matching, update the real item header prototypes at the same time as the body instead of leaving `UNK_RET`, `UNK_PARAMS`, or broad integer parameters.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itseakneedleheld.h changes `UNK_RET it_802B18B0(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void it_802B18B0(Item_GObj* gobj)`

decomp_lessons.lesson: If data ownership changes are required for a matched item unit, represent them through split and symbol metadata edits rather than compensating in source with artificial globals.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot reports data sections for this unit at 100%

naming_conventions.name: itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars
naming_conventions.convention: Character item private variable structs use `it<CharacterOrItem>_ItemVars` style and are added next to related item-var structs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itCharItems.h adds `typedef struct itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars` near `itSeakNeedleThrown_ItemVars`

naming_conventions.name: owner
naming_conventions.convention: Use a semantic field name for a recovered owner fighter pointer when the code stores and validates the parent fighter GObj through that field.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: owner is set from `parent_gobj` and compared against `ip->owner`

naming_conventions.name: parent_gobj, cur_pos, part, kind, facing_dir
naming_conventions.convention: Recovered item-spawn helper signatures can use role names for clear parameters instead of anonymous or broad types.
Local data labels may replace address-style global symbols when symbol metadata indicates local `.sdata` or `.sdata2` ownership.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt changes global `it_804D5468`, `it_804D5470`, `it_804DCF88` to local labels

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use `PAD_STACK` narrowly inside matched functions where stack shape required it.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR slice does not include a reviewer discussion explaining the PAD_STACK necessity; this is evidence of use, not a general endorsement.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a natural counted loop for repeated JObj needle visibility updates.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)` loop with HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll/HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll and HSD_JObjGetNext

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Handle Sheik and Kirby held-needle variants through an `ItemKind` switch that calls the corresponding fighter helper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: switches on `It_Kind_Seak_NeedleHeld` and `It_Kind_Kirby_SeakNeedleHeld` in it_802B18B0 and itSeakneedleheld_UnkMotion0_Anim

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use project item and JObj helpers (`GET_ITEM`, `GET_JOBJ`, `HSD_JObjGetChild`, `HSD_JObjGetNext`, `HSD_JObjSetScale`) in the recovered source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: includes `it/inlines.h` and `<baselib/jobj.h>` and uses helper calls in the implementation

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Mark the object Matching only after the code and data sections for the unit report 100% in the PR bot output.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py switches object to Matching
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports 6 new matches including three functions and data sections

review_feedback.author: IsaacGemal
review_feedback.text: The author disclosed being new to reverse decompilation and C, using Claude/Codex/Cursor plus ASM review and small functions to reach 100%, and expected stylistic changes or nuances might need addressing.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Maintainer response said nothing was too out of place, offered to clean it up for study, and thanked the contributor.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Bot reported +860 matched code bytes, +944 linked code bytes, and 6 new matches for the unit including three functions and `.data`, `.sdata`, `.sdata2`.

follow_up_queries: Find later cleanup commits touching `src/melee/it/items/itseakneedleheld.c` after ribbanya's cleanup comment.
Search for other item PRs adding `*_ItemVars` structs and union arms while matching item functions.
Search for PRs where `.sdata` or `.sdata2` globals were changed to local labels during item matching.
Search for PAD_STACK usage in matched item spawn helpers to compare whether these stack pads were later removed or justified.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR #2215 merged metadata and changed file list
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for 7 changed files
notes: The slice includes no GitHub review comments and no detailed maintainer code review, only issue comments.
The bot report supports matched-section results, but the intake did not independently run builds, objdiff, or regression checks.
The maintainer said they would clean it up, but the loaded slice does not show what cleanup was performed before merge.
PAD_STACK uses are visible and correlated with a bot match report, but the PR evidence does not explain why they were required.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover typed item-var owner fields when item matching proves them
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2215 shows held needle matching using a new `itSeakNeedleHeld_ItemVars` struct with `Fighter_GObj* owner` and a `seakneedleheld` union arm, replacing opaque DD4 item-var access in the matched source.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes should be updated when item function bodies are recovered
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2215 updates `itseakneedleheld.h` prototypes from unknown or broad types to the recovered typed signatures at the same time the functions are matched.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use metadata edits for local data-section ownership during a code match
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2215 adjusted `symbols.txt` local labels and `splits.txt` section ranges for `.sdata` and `.sdata2` while matching `itseakneedleheld.c`, with bot output reporting the affected data sections at 100%.

source_update_candidates.title: `itseakneedleheld.c` owns local `.sdata` and `.sdata2` labels in this split range
source_update_candidates.text: For PR #2215, `config/GALE01/splits.txt` changed `melee/it/items/itseakneedleheld.c` `.sdata` to `0x804D5468..0x804D5475` and `.sdata2` to `0x804DCF88..0x804DCF8C`, while `symbols.txt` changed `0x804D5468`, `0x804D5470`, and `0x804DCF88` from global address-style symbols to local labels.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source-owned path/split fact and should be curated separately rather than treated as a general lesson.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not overgeneralize AI-assisted PR disclosure into acceptance criteria
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2215 includes an author disclosure that much of the work used Claude/Codex/Cursor plus ASM review, and a maintainer replied that nothing was too out of place. If standards mention AI-assisted decomp, they should require normal evidence and review rather than treating this PR as proof that AI output alone is acceptable.
source_update_candidates.reason: The comments may be useful curator context, but they do not establish a technical matching standard by themselves.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all AI-assisted decompilation is accepted from this PR; the evidence only shows one author disclosure and a positive maintainer comment.
Do not promote PAD_STACK as normal style from this PR; two uses are visible and the bot report shows matching, but no review explanation or independent validation is included.
Do not infer semantics for all local labels `@224`, `@225`, or `@186` beyond the symbol metadata shown in the diff.
Do not claim exact cleanup changes by ribbanya; the loaded PR slice only contains a comment saying cleanup would be done.
